name: Hundido de Armallones Loop
name_original: Hundido de Armallones — circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
description: Hike the difficult 14.6-mile Hundido de Armallones circular route in Alto Tajo Natural Park, gaining 1736 feet over 6 hours and 40 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Armallones are situated within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, characterized by dramatic river gorges carved by the Tajo River. The landscape features significant geological formations, including twisted rock strata and large rockfalls, alongside extensive pine forests. This region offers a diverse terrain for hikers, ranging from river-level paths to elevated viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Armallones The most popular hiking route is Pico del Alar – circular by Salinas de la Inesperada from Ocentejo – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park and passes historical salt mines. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hundido de Armallones Loop, a difficult 14.6 miles (23.4 km) path. This challenging route offers extensive views of the Tajo River gorge and its unique geological features. Local hikers also love the Rambla del Avellano – circular desde Arbeteta – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Armallones is defined by deep river gorges, pine forests, and unique geological formations along the Tajo River. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Armallones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Armallones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Armallones?
A: Armallones offers a selection of &lt;strong&gt;over 10 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt;, providing diverse options within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Armallones?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available. The region research also highlights a roughly 11-kilometer (round trip) route starting from Ocentejo to the Hundido de Armallones and the Salinas de la Inesperada, which is often described as suitable for families.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Armallones?
A: Hikers in Armallones will encounter dramatic river gorges carved by the Tajo River, extensive pine forests, and unique geological formations. The area is renowned for its twisted rock strata and large rockfalls, particularly visible in the Hundido de Armallones, offering a 'master class in live geology'.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Armallones?
A: Yes, Armallones features several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113862557/pico-del-alar-circular-by-salinas-de-la-inesperada-from-ocentejo-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alar – circular by Salinas de la Inesperada from Ocentejo – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.8-mile trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120710465/rambla-del-avellano-circular-desde-arbeteta-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla del Avellano – circular desde Arbeteta – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 miles long.

Q: What is the Hundido de Armallones and how can I experience it?
A: The Hundido de Armallones is a breathtaking gorge carved by the Tajo River, famous for its immense rock formations and geological displays. You can experience its grandeur on the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027544487/hundido-de-armallones-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundido de Armallones Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.6-mile route offering extensive views of the gorge and its unique features.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, as you hike past the Hundido de Armallones, you can reach the &lt;strong&gt;Salinas de la Inesperada&lt;/strong&gt;, historical salt mines dating back to 1860, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure. The nearby village of Ocentejo also features a parish church, a castle, and a medieval bridge.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or natural swimming spots near Armallones hikes?
A: Yes, the Tajo River creates dynamic environments with tranquil bathing areas and natural pools, especially in good weather, where you can find turquoise waters. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7422569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escaleruela Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural monument in the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Armallones?
A: The routes in Armallones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.0 stars from over 7 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the dramatic geological features, the beauty of the Tajo River, and the diverse terrain within the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any caves or unique natural monuments to explore?
A: Yes, the region offers unique natural monuments. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima de Alcorón Cave&lt;/a&gt; and admire the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5610581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Arco&lt;/a&gt;, a natural arch formation.

Q: What is the best way to access the hiking trails in Armallones?
A: The village of Ocentejo often serves as a common starting point and 'gateway' to the Hundido de Armallones and other trails. Many routes begin from or pass through local villages, making them accessible for hikers.

Q: Are there any trails that offer particularly good views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027544487/hundido-de-armallones-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundido de Armallones Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive views of the Tajo River gorge and its unique geological features. Many trails also offer stunning views of the canyon from above before descending to river level.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other activities in Armallones?
A: Yes, beyond hiking, some trails are also suitable for mountain biking and trail running. The Tajo River itself offers opportunities for recreation, with its rapids and tranquil bathing areas.
tips: The Alto Tajo protected area is a place with little crowds but of great scenic value. A clear example is the Hundido de Armallones area, surrounded by an impressive landscape that also offers some natural pools on the Tagus riverbed. They are ideal for cooling off in the summer months. It can be accessed from the west bank, although the views from this side are equally wonderful.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Hundido de Armallones circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as difficult by komoot, covering approximately 23.4 kilometers with over 500 meters of elevation gain and descent. While some sections are relatively flat, expect significant uphill stretches and descents into the canyon, requiring good physical condition and adequate hydration.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is estimated to take around 6 hours and 40 minutes to complete, not including stops for rest or enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: Where does the Hundido de Armallones circular trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Ocentejo. Ocentejo serves as a good base, and you should be able to find parking within or near the village. It also has a tourist information office.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, ranging from wide forest tracks to paths literally excavated beneath rocky overhangs. You'll traverse a deep canyon carved by the Tajo River, encountering impressive rocky formations and vertical walls. Expect both level sections and notable ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The most prominent feature is the Hundido de Armallones itself, a spectacular gorge formed by a massive 16th-century rockslide. You'll see huge rock formations in the riverbed, creating rapids and beautiful natural pools with striking turquoise waters. The trail also offers elevated viewpoints overlooking the canyon and stretches alongside the vibrant Tajo River.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for birds of prey such as Bonelli's eagle, Egyptian vulture, griffon vulture, and peregrine falcon nesting in the vertical rock faces. Along the river, you might spot otters, kingfishers, and common trout.

faqs.question: Is the Hundido de Armallones circular route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the Alto Tajo Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife, especially ground-nesting birds and other sensitive species. Given the trail's length and difficulty, ensure your dog is fit for a long hike and bring plenty of water for them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, making hydration critical, while winters can bring colder conditions. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Hundido de Armallones trail within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, especially regarding wildlife protection and fire prevention. During certain sensitive breeding seasons, access to specific areas might be restricted, so it's wise to check with the local tourist office in Ocentejo beforehand.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo, the Georuta 1 «Ocentejo-Hundido de Armallones», and the Ruta Hundido de Armallones a Salinas y Pico Alar.

faqs.question: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the turquoise waters of the Tajo River, particularly in the areas where the 16th-century rockslide created natural pools, are ideal for swimming. These spots offer a refreshing break, especially during warmer months.
